From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American mystery drama television series

| *The Cosby Mysteries* | |

| Composers | [Craig Handy](/wiki/Craig_Handy "Craig Handy") [Bill Cosby](/wiki/Bill_Cosby "Bill Cosby") (as William H. Cosby, Jr.) [David Weiss](/wiki/David_Weiss_(musician) "David Weiss (musician)") |

***The Cosby Mysteries*** is an American [mystery](/wiki/Mystery_fiction "Mystery fiction") [drama](/wiki/Drama_(film_and_television) "Drama (film and television)") television series starring [Bill Cosby](/wiki/Bill_Cosby "Bill Cosby") that aired on [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") from September 21, 1994, to April 12, 1995. 19 episodes were made. It was the first television series to star Cosby since *[The Cosby Show](/wiki/The_Cosby_Show "The Cosby Show")* (which ended in the spring of 1992) and lasted just one season (1994–1995).[[1]](#cite_note-1) Actor/rapper [Yasiin Bey](/wiki/Yasiin_Bey "Yasiin Bey") (also known as Mos Def) appeared in several episodes (sometimes credited as Dante Smith).[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Premise

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cosby_Mysteries&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Premise")]

Cosby played Guy Hanks, a [New York City Police Department](/wiki/New_York_City_Police_Department "New York City Police Department") [criminalist](/wiki/Forensic_science "Forensic science"), who retired from the police force after winning $44 million in the lottery.

His peaceful retirement is frequently interrupted by his former colleagues, Detective Adam Sully ([James Naughton](/wiki/James_Naughton "James Naughton")) and medical examiner John Chapman ([Robert Stanton](/wiki/Robert_Stanton_(actor) "Robert Stanton (actor)")), who ask him to consult on tough cases. As Hanks uses his wits and his forensics knowledge to solve crimes, he deals with his holistic housekeeper Angie Corea ([Rita Moreno](/wiki/Rita_Moreno "Rita Moreno")) and his girlfriend Barbara Lorenz ([Lynn Whitfield](/wiki/Lynn_Whitfield "Lynn Whitfield")).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Production history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cosby_Mysteries&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Production history")]

The show was created by David Black and [William Link](/wiki/William_Link "William Link"). Link's previous series included *[Columbo](/wiki/Columbo_(TV_series) "Columbo (TV series)")* on NBC and *[Murder, She Wrote](/wiki/Murder,_She_Wrote "Murder, She Wrote")* on CBS. Link developed the series at Cosby's request, as Cosby wanted to make an intelligent, character-driven mystery series that did not rely on graphic violence.

The show began on January 31, 1994, as a two hour movie, and 18 regular episodes began airing on NBC on September 21, 1994. The series ranked 62nd for the season with an average 9.9 household rating.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Executive producer William Link criticized NBC for not effectively advertising the show before its premiere launch. NBC Entertainment president [Warren Littlefield](/wiki/Warren_Littlefield "Warren Littlefield") blamed Bill Cosby for not using his personal fame to promote the show.

At the beginning of 1995, prior to the show's cancellation, creators William Link and David Black were dropped from the production team by NBC.[[5]](#cite_note-latimes_35153-5)

Critics expressed hope that *The Cosby Mysteries* would fare better than Cosby's previous two ventures,[[6]](#cite_note-6) *[Here and Now](/wiki/Here_and_Now_(1992_TV_series) "Here and Now (1992 TV series)")* and the game show remake, *[You Bet Your Life](/wiki/You_Bet_Your_Life#1992.E2.80.931993 "You Bet Your Life")*. *The Cosby Mysteries* has been rerun in the United Kingdom, on digital channel [ITV1](/wiki/ITV1 "ITV1"), and in the United States on [A&E](/wiki/A%26E_Network "A&E Network"), [STARZ! Mystery](/wiki/Starz_Encore "Starz Encore") and [TV One](/wiki/TV_One_(US_TV_network) "TV One (US TV network)").

*The Cosby Mysteries* was shot in New York City by SAH Enterprises.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Cancellation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cosby_Mysteries&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cancellation")]

*The Cosby Mysteries* lasted 19 episodes in one season, and was cancelled permanently in February 1995.[[5]](#cite_note-latimes_35153-5) On April 12, 1995, *The Cosby Mysteries* ceased operations for good.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cosby_Mysteries&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Bill Cosby](/wiki/Bill_Cosby "Bill Cosby") as Guy Hanks
* [Rita Moreno](/wiki/Rita_Moreno "Rita Moreno") as Angie Corea
* [Robert Stanton](/wiki/Robert_Stanton_(actor) "Robert Stanton (actor)") as Medical Examiner John Chapman
* [James Naughton](/wiki/James_Naughton "James Naughton") as Det. Adam Scully
* [Yasiin Bey](/wiki/Yasiin_Bey "Yasiin Bey") as Dante Beze
* [Lynn Whitfield](/wiki/Lynn_Whitfield "Lynn Whitfield") as Barbara Lorenz

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Cosby_Mysteries&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

* In an episode of *[The Simpsons](/wiki/The_Simpsons "The Simpsons")*, "[Guess Who's Coming to Criticize Dinner?](/wiki/Guess_Who%27s_Coming_to_Criticize_Dinner%3F "Guess Who's Coming to Criticize Dinner?")", [Homer Simpson](/wiki/Homer_Simpson "Homer Simpson") laments the cancellation of *The Cosby Mysteries*, saying "That show had limitless possibilities!"
* An episode of *[Saturday Night Live](/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")* hosted by [Patrick Stewart](/wiki/Patrick_Stewart "Patrick Stewart") on February 5, 1994, did a parody sketch of *The Cosby Mysteries*. [Adam Sandler](/wiki/Adam_Sandler "Adam Sandler") played Cosby as a buffoonish character, who rambles on incoherently with dialog full of made up nonsense words.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
